fields.description:
Wire the two-scope gate posture across all three gates, plumb the resolution-class + sanitized diagnostic through the verdict-returning gates, define the exit-11 contract, own the shared-file (runner.py) reconciliation, enforce never-certify structurally, and document the model. This story lands LAST (wave 3) and is the INTEGRATION OWNER for the seams the other children touch.

## Why
The three gates must share a systemic *block-but-retryable* posture while PRESERVING per-criterion fail-open, carry `resolution_class` + `diagnostic` so routing and troubleshooting both work, expose a distinct-but-grounded exit code, never sign a degraded result, and tell the human exactly what to do.

## What

### Two-scope posture
A **systemic** failure â†’ block-but-retryable (exit 11, unsigned) across the gates; a **per-criterion** failure â†’ drop-and-continue fail-open (one flaky finder among N must not block a claim â€” PRESERVED). Make code-review match plan-review's existing systemic block; turn completion's fail-closed-as-FAIL into a distinct retryable exit (NOT a verification FAIL, which means "your work is wrong").

### Two data-flow shapes (the gates surface failure differently)
- **Shape A â€” plan-review + code-review return a DEGRADED VERDICT DICT.** The degrade helper sets `coverage.resolution_class` + `coverage.diagnostic` in the dict; the CLI reads `verdict['coverage'].get('resolution_class')`.
  - **Write-site:** `gate_dispatch._degraded_plan_review_verdict` and `_degraded_code_review_verdict` read `outcome = getattr(error, 'outcome', None)` off the caught error (mamba attaches `.outcome` at the runner's generic except seam) and, when present, set `coverage['resolution_class'] = outcome.resolution_class.value` and `coverage['diagnostic'] = outcome.diagnostic`.
  - **Reachability:** these helpers are the degrade path called by `produce_plan_review_verdict` / `produce_code_review_verdict` on a systemic outage (pre-flight AND mid-run); the CLI arms below consume their returned dict.
- **Shape B â€” completion-verify FAILS by RAISING `LLMUnavailableError`** (no verdict dict). The crux: `transition_close._completion_precheck` (line ~174) has a broad `except Exception as exc: raise CommandError(...) from None` that TODAY discards the original exception and its `.outcome`. Fix: in `_completion_precheck`, read `_outcome = getattr(exc, 'outcome', None)` BEFORE constructing the `CommandError`, and thread it through so the close CLI can map it. Mechanism: give `CommandError` an optional `outcome` attribute (or a `RetryableCommandError` subclass); set `err.outcome = _outcome` before re-raising; the `close_ticket` CLI path then reads `getattr(err, 'outcome', None)` and maps a retryable outcome â†’ exit 11 (else the existing fail-closed exit 1).

### Exit-code-11 contract
Retryable classes (`WAIT_AND_RETRY` / `RETRY_NOW`) â†’ exit **11** ("transient â€” retry", NEW code). Any other non-null disposition â†’ the gate's EXISTING INDETERMINATE exit. A null disposition â†’ the existing PASS/BLOCK exit.
- **Exit 2 is NOT redefined.** `review-plan` ALREADY exits 2 for INDETERMINATE (pinned by arawana's RED baseline); that use predates this story and is UNCHANGED. Only the *retryable subset* is peeled off to the new exit 11.
- **CLI sites (correct handlers in `_llm_commands.py`):** `rebar review-plan` (`_review_plan()`, shape A, currently returns `0/1/2` at its tail) and `rebar review-code` (`_review_code()`, shape A â€” the code-review gate that returns `code_review_verdict`; NOT `rebar review`/`_review()`, which is the per-ticket `review_ticket` advisory reviewer) read shape A; `rebar verify-completion` (`_verify_completion()`) and the completion close gate in `transition_close.py` read shape B.
- **Worked example:** a 429-driven degraded plan-review carries `coverage.resolution_class='WAIT_AND_RETRY'` â†’ exits 11; a 429 during completion close raises `LLMUnavailableError`, `_completion_precheck` reads its `.outcome.resolution_class=='WAIT_AND_RETRY'` â†’ the close command exits 11 (fail-closed, unsigned).

### Per-class actionable messages (ResolutionClass â†’ human-facing text)
| ResolutionClass | Message |
|---|---|
| `WAIT_AND_RETRY` | Transient provider overload/rate-limit â€” wait for the backoff window, then retry. |
| `RETRY_NOW` | Transient connection blip â€” retry immediately. |
| `INCREASE_PROVIDER_LIMITS` | Provider usage/quota ceiling hit â€” raise the provider limit or wait for the reset. |
| `CHANGE_SETTINGS` | The request exceeded a configured bound (tokens/steps/timeout) â€” adjust the setting and retry. |
| `CHANGE_INPUT` | The input was rejected (too large / malformed) â€” reduce or fix it and retry. |
| `CHANGE_PROVIDER_OR_MODEL` | The model/provider is unavailable or refused â€” switch model/provider. |
| `FIX_AGENT_DESIGN` | An agent-construction bug (no tools / bad output contract) â€” fix the op wiring. |
| `NEEDS_INVESTIGATION` | Unclassified failure â€” inspect the sanitized diagnostic. |
The retryable pair (WAIT_AND_RETRY/RETRY_NOW) drive exit 11; the rest map to the existing INDETERMINATE exit.

### Verdict-schema field (both verdict-returning gates)
Add OPTIONAL `coverage.resolution_class` (enum, the 8 closed values) + `coverage.diagnostic` (sanitized object) to `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` and `code_review_verdict.schema.json` (their `coverage` is already an open object, so this is a documentary property definition; readers already tolerate unknown coverage keys â€” forward/rollback-safe, no scrub/migration). **`completion_verdict.schema.json` is NOT changed:** completion surfaces failure via the RAISED `LLMUnavailableError.outcome` (shape B), not a verdict dict, so a schema field there would be dead (YAGNI). This story is the sole writer of the schema edits.

### Session-log diagnostic write (calling convention)
At each shape-A degrade site, after building the sanitized diagnostic, call `rebar.append_session_log(entry, summary=..., relates_to=<ticket_id>)` where `entry` is `json.dumps(sanitized_diagnostic, sort_keys=True)` (the sanitized dict, NOT the raw exception), `summary` is `f"llm-degrade {resolution_class.value} on <gate>"`, and `relates_to` is the reviewed ticket id when available (best-effort; a session-log failure must never fail the gate).

### Never-sign guard (structural)
A degraded/INDETERMINATE verdict must never be signed. Today only a `certifiable` PASS is signed, so INDETERMINATE is already unsigned incidentally; this guard makes it STRUCTURAL: at the sign decision (the `review_plan` / completion sign path), refuse to sign when `verdict.verdict != "PASS"` or `verdict.coverage.get("resolution_class")` is set. Additionally, re-check the material fingerprint IMMEDIATELY before signing (retries + timeouts widen the verifyâ†’sign window; avoid attesting drifted state) â€” compare the head/material fingerprint captured at verify time against a fresh read in `sign_manifest`/the sign path and refuse on mismatch. Verify stays strictly OUTSIDE the write lock (`transition_close` current ordering preserved).

### Integration ownership (shared files)
`src/rebar/llm/runner.py` was modified by five children at distinct-but-adjacent seams; this story owns the final runner.py reconciliation, the single-source-of-truth config namespace, the verdict-schema edits, and the taxonomy/retry/timeout docs. It lands LAST.

## Scope
- `gate_dispatch.py` degrade write-sites; `transition_close.py` `_completion_precheck` (`.outcome` thread-through, exit 11) preserving verify-outside-lock ordering + pre-sign fingerprint re-check; `_llm_commands.py` exit-11 mapping (review-plan/review-code/verify-completion); `signing.py` never-sign guard + fingerprint recheck; the two verdict JSON schemas; `runner.py` reconciliation; `docs/` + `docs/adr/`.
- NOT in scope: removing or weakening per-criterion fail-open (must be preserved); changing `completion_verdict.schema.json`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar review-plan` (`_review_plan()`) exits 11 when `coverage.resolution_class` is `WAIT_AND_RETRY` or `RETRY_NOW`.
- [ ] `rebar review-plan` exits 2 (the existing INDETERMINATE code) when the verdict is INDETERMINATE with a non-retryable disposition.
- [ ] `rebar review-code` (`_review_code()`, the code-review gate) applies the same shape-A exit-11 mapping.
- [ ] The completion close gate (`transition_close._completion_precheck`) threads the caught `LLMUnavailableError.outcome` through the `CommandError` and exits 11 when it is retryable.
- [ ] The completion close gate exits 1 (fail-closed) when the caught exception carries no retryable outcome.
- [ ] A degraded / INDETERMINATE verdict is emitted unsigned (no attestation written).
- [ ] A degraded verdict/close carries the class-specific message from the ResolutionClassâ†’message table.
- [ ] `docs/exit-codes.md` gains a recorded-decision entry defining exit 11 (block-but-retryable).
- [ ] `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py` pins exit 11.
- [ ] The exit-11 ADR enumerates the concrete consumers â€” the driving agent/human (docs/exit-codes.md), CI (`.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`, `test.yml`; any-non-zero-is-failure, never invokes the gate CLIs so exit 11 is invisible to the Verified gate), and the review-bot (`src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py`; votes off the verdict dict, tolerates open coverage keys) â€” confirms each additive-safe, and states the rollback (retryable falls back to the existing INDETERMINATE exit; no data migration).
- [ ] Per-criterion fail-open is preserved: a single flaky finder among N does not block (regression test).
- [ ] `coverage.resolution_class` (enum, 8 closed values) is an optional property on `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` and `code_review_verdict.schema.json`.
- [ ] `coverage.diagnostic` (sanitized object) is an optional property on those two schemas.
- [ ] The shape-A degrade write-site copies `resolution_class` / `diagnostic` from the caught error's `.outcome` into `coverage`.
- [ ] The sanitized diagnostic is written to the session log via `append_session_log(json.dumps(diagnostic, sort_keys=True), summary=..., relates_to=<ticket>)` at the degrade site (best-effort; never fails the gate).
- [ ] The structural never-sign guard rejects signing any verdict whose `verdict != PASS` or whose `coverage.resolution_class` is set (regression test).
- [ ] The material fingerprint is re-checked immediately before signing; a mismatch refuses to sign (test for the mismatch path).
- [ ] Verify remains outside the write lock (ordering in `transition_close` preserved; asserted by a test).
- [ ] The exit-11 ADR is authored with sections: Context, Decision (11 = block-but-retryable, distinct from 2/INDETERMINATE), Consumers + additive-safety, Rollback.
- [ ] The diagnostic-sanitization ADR is authored with sections: Context (why redact), Allowlist + redaction rules, Threat model (what must never leak: keys/PII/hex/base64/email), Test coverage.
- [ ] The write-gated MCP LLM surface returns a structured failure result (never a raw raise) carrying the disposition, with a test for a degraded sub-path.
- [ ] `make lint`, `make typecheck`, `make test` are all green.

## Verified structural facts (experiment log â€” repo snapshot, offline)
```
Shape-B site: src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py :: _completion_precheck (line ~174 broad except re-wraps to CommandError from None â€” discards .outcome; the fix threads it).
Shape-A sites: src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py :: _degraded_plan_review_verdict / _degraded_code_review_verdict (read error.outcome).
CLI arms: src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py :: _review_plan (tail returns 0/1/2), _review_code (code-review gate), _verify_completion. (_review is review_ticket â€” NOT a site.)
Verdict schemas (coverage already additionalProperties:true): plan_review_verdict.schema.json, code_review_verdict.schema.json (edited); completion_verdict.schema.json has NO coverage object and is NOT edited (shape B).
Exit-code contract: docs/exit-codes.md (0/1/2/10 frozen 2026-06-09), pinned by tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py. 11 is additive.
Signing: src/rebar/signing.py :: sign_manifest (+ surfaced via __init__.py / _mcp_writes.py). Never-sign guard + pre-sign fingerprint recheck attach here; verify stays OUTSIDE the write lock.
Consumers: CI = .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml + test.yml (run make targets, never the gate CLIs). Review-bot = src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py (consumes verdict dict, not exit codes).
```
Additive/rollback-safe: schema fields optional; exit 11 additive (any-non-zero-is-failure consumers unaffected). This story records the two NEW ADRs (exit-11 contract, diagnostic-sanitization) â€” the transport-retry (0037) and async-watchdog (0038) ADRs already exist â€” and owns the runner.py reconciliation, landing LAST.
